Nx Witness REST API documentation confirms /rest/v1/devices is the standard endpoint for device management. Source: https://support.networkoptix.com/hc/en-us/articles/32766279894679-The-HTTP-REST-API

pi_items.key: API method for updating device properties
pi_items.source: Nx Witness REST API documentation confirms PATCH method is used for device updates. Source: https://support.networkoptix.com/hc/en-us/articles/32766279894679-The-HTTP-REST-API

Nx Witness API uses deprecated minArchiveDays/maxArchiveDays (in days) but updated to minArchivePeriodS/maxArchivePeriodS (in seconds). Web search confirmed existence of these fields in Nx Witness API.
Confirmed in task setup scripts and Nx Witness VMS environment setup documentation. Authentication method uses Bearer token returned from login endpoint.
Specified in both task.json metadata and referenced in export_result.sh script at line 19 (REPORT_PATH variable)

pi_items.verified_value: Retention periods follow a tiered approach based on camera location sensitivity: critical infrastructure (Server Room: 180-365d) > public areas (Entrance/Lobby: 60-180d) > external/loading (Loading Dock/Parking: 30-90d)
pi_items.source: Analysis of metadata retention values shows consistent pattern where more sensitive locations have longer retention requirements

privileged_info_summary: The task configures retention policies on five cameras in a Nx Witness VMS system using the REST API with Bearer token authentication. All metadata retention values (minArchivePeriodS and maxArchivePeriodS) are correctly converted from day-based durations to seconds, with values ranging from 2,592,000 seconds (30 days minimum) for the Parking Lot Camera to 31,536,000 seconds (365 days maximum) for the Server Room Camera. The API endpoints (/rest/v1/devices, /rest/v1/login/sessions) and field names are verified as real Nx Witness API properties. The data is synthetic, created programmatically with Nx Witness's testcamera tool. The task requires agents to authenticate, retrieve device IDs, apply PATCH requests to set archive retention periods, and generate a JSON report at /home/ga/retention_policy_report.json.
